Entry-Level Biology Interactive Assessment

Microscope Mystery:
What Am I Looking At?

Analyze randomized microscope mystery samples, make evidence-based claims, and download your completed report as a PDF to submit in Canvas.

1. Observe

Look for visible clues in each sample card.

2. Claim + Evidence

Classify each sample and explain your reasoning.

3. Download PDF

Submit the generated report in Canvas.

Purpose

Biologists use microscopes to observe cells and make evidence-based conclusions. In this activity, you will examine three unknown samples and decide what type of cell or tissue you are likely viewing.

Your goal: Do not just name the sample. Use specific observations to support your claim.

What you will submit

You will complete each section in this tool, generate a report, download the PDF, and upload the PDF to the Canvas assignment.

What your report must include
  • At least three observations for each sample
  • A cell type claim for each sample
  • Evidence and reasoning that support each claim
  • One structure-function explanation for each sample
  • An explanation of why microscopes are needed
  • An explanation of surface-area-to-volume ratio
  • An explanation of how the plasma membrane regulates movement
  • A final reflection on the hardest sample to identify

Generate Your Mystery Set

Enter your name, then generate your three randomized mystery samples. Your set will be saved in this browser.

Big Idea Questions

Use complete sentences and connect your answers to cells, membranes, and the samples you analyzed.

Preview + Download PDF

Review your report before downloading. Submit the downloaded PDF in Canvas.

Microscope Mystery interactive assessment • Save often if you are not completing this in one sitting.
